source_quote: No significant difference was found on pain... VAS (p < 0.01, within-subject; p = 0.63, interaction; p = 0.77, between-subject). VAS means and SDs reported only in figures, not extractable from text.
YE vs NE KOOS Pain: mean difference of 22.9 [95% CI, 6.9 to 38.8; p = 0.003]. YE follow-up 70.3+/-12.8 (n=10), NE follow-up 49.9+/-24.7 (n=10). KOOS Pain /100, higher=less pain.

outcome: Spontaneous vaginal birth (as defined by trial authors)
source_quote: spontaneous vaginal birth (761 [69.1%] vs 372 [69%]; RR 1.03, 95% CI 0.96, 1.10)
caesarean birth (163 [14.8%] vs 84 [15.2%]; relative risk (RR) 0.97, 95% CI 0.76, 1.24)
